title: bis / working paper / labour market flows | Research Paper | Labour market flows, unemployment and the Phillips curve
summary: BIS Working Paper No 1341 introduces a new "unemployment gap" measure that distinguishes between stock-based unemployment (traditional point-in-time count) and flow-based unemployment (rate implied by current labour market transitions). The paper demonstrates empirically using US data that this gap provides better predictive power for future inflation and wage dynamics than traditional slack measures. A theoretical search-and-matching model with nominal wage rigidities and persistent shocks explains why labour market transition flows matter for price and wage behaviour.
